title: Eriostomum lanatum
intro: Eriostomum lanatum, also known as Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America, characterized by its bright red berries and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and diarrhea, due to its astringent and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriostomum lanatum include arbutin, hydroquinone, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eriostomum lanatum include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to promote urinary health and prevent infections.
taxonomy: Eriostomum lanatum is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Lentibulariaceae. The family Lentibulariaceae is a group of carnivorous plants commonly known as bladdersworts. The genus Eriostomum is a small group of plants within this family. The classification of Eriostomum lanatum is further confirmed by its botanical name.
